question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an arbitrary `fileId` value (e.g., a non-standard address book file other than 102) to `findNetworkNodes()` to retrieve a different address book's node set with altered `reward_rate` values, causing the caller to compute transaction fees against out-of-design fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a crafted `orderDirection` string value other than 'ASC' or 'DESC' into the native SQL CASE expression `case when :orderDirection = 'ASC' then abe.node_id end asc` to inject SQL and manipulate the `reward_rate` values returned, causing fee calculations to use attacker-influenced data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `limit` value larger than `MAX_LIMIT` (25) directly to `findNetworkNodes()` if the service layer's `getEffectiveLimit()` cap is bypassed, causing the query to return more node fee data than design parameters allow and enabling bulk enumeration of `reward_rate` values?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a very large `nodeIds` array to `findNetworkNodes()` such that the `coalesce(array_length(:nodeIds, 1), 0) = 0 or abe.node_id = any(:nodeIds)` condition is evaluated with thousands of entries, causing a resource exhaustion that disrupts fee schedule lookups for legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user set `minNodeId` equal to `maxNodeId` to a specific node ID and observe the `rewardRateStart` (mapped from `ns.reward_rate`) for that single node, then use this information to predict and game transaction fee calculations for transactions routed to that node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `latest_node_stake` CTE which selects `where consensus_timestamp = (select max(consensus_timestamp) from node_stake)` by timing requests during a node stake update window to observe transitional `reward_rate` values that differ from the finalized fee parameters, enabling fee manipulation through timing attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `minNodeId` as a negative value or `Long.MIN_VALUE` to the `abe.node_id >= :minNodeId` filter, causing the query to return nodes outside the intended node ID range and exposing `reward_rate` data for nodes not meant to be publicly visible?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `maxNodeId` as `Long.MAX_VALUE` combined with a very large `nodeIds` array to force the `abe.node_id = any(:nodeIds)` branch to scan all nodes, leaking the full set of `reward_rate` values and enabling an attacker to identify nodes with anomalous fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `nullif(ns.max_stake, -1) as maxStake` sentinel value logic by observing when `maxStake` returns null (indicating `max_stake = -1`) to infer that a node's stake ceiling is uncapped, then use this to predict which nodes will have higher `reward_rate` and thus higher transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `nullif(ns.min_stake, -1) as minStake` sentinel value logic to identify nodes where `min_stake = -1` (returned as null), inferring that those nodes have no minimum stake requirement and thus always earn `reward_rate` fees regardless of stake, enabling targeted fee manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user observe the `declineReward` field returned by the query (from `n.decline_reward`) to identify which nodes are not collecting staking rewards, then use this information to selectively stake to reward-collecting nodes and manipulate the effective fee distribution across the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly poll `findNetworkNodes()` with different `fileId` values to enumerate all address books and their associated `reward_rate` values, building a complete map of fee parameters across all address book versions to identify historical fee anomalies?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `coalesce(n.account_id, abe.node_account_id) as nodeAccountId` fallback logic to identify nodes where the `node` table entry is missing (returning `abe.node_account_id`), indicating nodes with potentially stale fee parameters not yet updated in the `node` table?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `fileId=101` (the other standard address book file) instead of the default `102` to retrieve a different set of nodes with different `reward_rate` values, causing fee estimation logic that relies on this endpoint to use out-of-design fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `left join latest_node_stake ns on abe.node_id = ns.node_id` left join behavior to identify nodes where `ns.reward_rate` is NULL (no stake data), causing downstream fee calculation code that doesn't handle NULL to default to zero fees or throw exceptions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with `nodeIds` containing duplicate entries to cause the `abe.node_id = any(:nodeIds)` condition to match the same node multiple times, potentially causing fee aggregation logic consuming this data to double-count `reward_rate` values?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `limit=0` or a negative `limit` to `findNetworkNodes()` to bypass the `limit :limit` clause behavior in PostgreSQL, potentially returning all rows and exposing the complete `reward_rate` dataset outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `stakeRewarded` and `stakeNotRewarded` fields returned by the query to calculate the exact ratio of rewarded vs non-rewarded stake per node, then use this to predict future `reward_rate` changes and front-run fee parameter updates?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user observe the `stakingPeriod` field (from `ns.staking_period`) returned by the query to determine the exact timing of staking period boundaries, then time transactions to exploit the transition between fee periods when `reward_rate` values change?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an extremely large `nodeIds` array (e.g., 10,000+ entries) to the `abe.node_id = any(:nodeIds)` condition to cause a PostgreSQL query plan regression that bypasses index usage, resulting in a full table scan that degrades fee lookup performance for all users?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `latest_address_book` CTE's `order by start_consensus_timestamp desc limit 1` to observe the `startConsensusTimestamp` and `endConsensusTimestamp` of the current address book, then use this timing information to predict when fee parameters will next be updated?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `fileId=0` or `fileId=-1` to `findNetworkNodes()` to trigger unexpected behavior in the `where file_id = :fileId` clause of the `latest_address_book` CTE, potentially returning an empty result set that causes fee calculation code to fall back to default (possibly insecure) fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `grpcProxyEndpoint` JSONB extraction `(n.grpc_proxy_endpoint->>'port')::integer` to observe node gRPC port configurations, then use this to directly connect to nodes and submit transactions that bypass the standard fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user observe the `adminKey` field returned by the query (from `n.admin_key`) to identify the public key of node administrators, then use this information to target social engineering attacks aimed at modifying node fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `serviceEndpointsJson` subquery that orders by `abse.ip_address_v4 asc, abse.port asc` to enumerate all service endpoint IP addresses and ports for each node, enabling targeted network-level attacks against specific nodes to disrupt their fee collection?
